attributes.description: Alerts when any Kubernetes node reports the MemoryPressure condition. This is a warning signal that the node is running low on memory and may begin evicting pods.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] Node memory pressure

investigation_guide.blob:
## Node Memory Pressure

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected node(s) from the alert results.
2. Open the Node Performance dashboard filtered to the affected node to see memory usage trends.
3. Navigate to Pod & Container Detail filtered by node name to find the top memory consumers.
4. Check for pods without memory limits or with very high limits.
5. Run `kubectl top pods --sort-by=memory -A` to see current memory consumption.

### Common Causes
- Memory-intensive workloads without proper limits
- Memory leaks in application containers
- Too many pods scheduled on the node
- Insufficient node memory capacity for the workload mix

### Escalation
If memory pressure persists, the node will begin evicting pods. Consider cordoning the node and migrating workloads.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.node.condition_memory_pressure IS NOT NULL
| STATS any_pressure = MAX(k8s.node.condition_memory_pressure)
    BY k8s.node.name
// Alert when memory pressure is active (1 = pressure detected)
| WHERE any_pressure == 1
| EVAL status = "MemoryPressure"
